_meta.description: Global pipeline configuration. Controls iteration limits, confirmation requirements, agent behavior, and escalation rules for the Figma component generation pipeline.
_meta.change: Redesigned iteration loop for 2–3 attempt convergence. Added pre-flight step, surgical fix mode, same-check escalation, and targeted fixActions.

figmaMCPGate.description: Hard rule: the pipeline exists to build a Figma design system. It cannot run in config-only mode. Config files are a byproduct of a successful Figma build — never the primary output.
figmaMCPGate.rule: STEP 0 (Figma MCP connectivity probe) MUST pass before any other step executes. If the Figma Desktop Bridge is not connected, the entire pipeline is blocked.
figmaMCPGate.connectivityProbe: figma_get_metadata({ fileKey: 'm2iOWX3I9aDI5kgyw4wCo0' }) — must return a non-null file name
figmaMCPGate.onProbeFailure: Output PIPELINE BLOCKED message with setup instructions. Stop immediately. Do not run any other step.
figmaMCPGate.configFileRule: Config files (tokens/components/*.json, docs/components/*.md) are ONLY generated in STEP 12 after ALL three conditions are true: (1) STEP 0 Figma connected, (2) STEP 7 component built with a real componentSetId, (3) STEP 11 publish confirmed. Generating config files without a live Figma component is forbidden.
figmaMCPGate.noConfigOnlyMode: There is no config-only mode, dry-run mode, or offline mode in this pipeline. Any execution without a Figma MCP connection is invalid and must be rejected with the PIPELINE BLOCKED message.

steps.name: Code Agent — read codebase
steps.note: Reads codebase/ for exact props/variants/states. PARTIAL or NOT_FOUND is acceptable — pipeline continues with Vision Agent fallback.

preFlightChecks.description: Run BEFORE Step 7 (Figma MCP execution). Failures here do NOT consume an iteration — they are infrastructure problems, not design problems. Fix and re-run pre-flight only.

checks.name: Fonts loaded
checks.action: await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: 'Zoho Puvi', style: 'Regular' }); await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: 'Zoho Puvi', style: 'Medium' }); await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: 'Zoho Puvi', style: 'Semibold' }); await figma.loadFontAsync({ family: 'Lato', style: 'Regular' });
checks.failMessage: Font load failed. Zoho Puvi or Lato not available. Check Figma file font access before continuing.

checks.name: Target page exists or can be created
checks.action: Check figma.root.children for a page matching spec.page. If missing, create it. Log creation.
checks.failMessage: Could not find or create target page.

checks.name: Variable IDs reachable
checks.action: For each variableId in the execution plan, call figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync(id). Confirm it returns a non-null variable object.
checks.failMessage: One or more Figma variable IDs are unreachable. Re-query figma-variable-bindings.json and confirm IDs match the current file.

checks.name: Library component keys valid
checks.action: For each variantKey in the plan, call figma_get_library_components and confirm the key appears in the results.
checks.failMessage: One or more variant keys not found in library. Re-query with figma_get_library_components to get current keys.

checks.name: Canvas space clear
checks.action: Call figma_capture_screenshot on the target page. Confirm clear space exists at the intended placement coordinates. If not, adjust coordinates.
checks.failMessage: Target coordinates overlap existing work. Find new clear space.

preFlightChecks.rule: ALL blockExecution=true checks must pass before Step 7 runs. Pre-flight failures do NOT count toward the iterationGuard.maxAttempts counter.

iterationGuard.scope: One iteration = pre-flight (Step 6) + execute (Step 7) + token audit (Step 8) + verify (Step 9). Pre-flight failures outside this scope do NOT count.
iterationGuard.fixModeDescription: On iteration failure, re-run ONLY the Figma MCP operations that fix the specific failed checks. Do NOT rebuild the entire component. Rebuilding resets all progress and risks introducing new issues.

sameCheckEscalation.rule: If the SAME check ID fails on two consecutive attempts, immediately escalate that check to human — do NOT spend attempt 3 on it.
sameCheckEscalation.output: PARTIAL ESCALATE — Check {checkId} ({checkName}) failed on attempts {n} and {n+1}. This check is escalated to human review. Continuing pipeline for remaining checks.

attempt1.onFail: Output failed check IDs with fixActions (from verify-rubric.json). Execute ONLY the fixActions for failed checks. Re-run token audit + screenshot verify.

attempt2.onFail: Output failed check IDs. Compare with attempt 1 failures. For any check that failed BOTH attempt 1 and 2 → escalate that check. For new failures → apply fixActions. Re-run token audit + screenshot verify.
attempt2.log: Attempt 2 failures: {failedCheckIds}. Repeated from attempt 1: {repeatedIds} (escalated). New failures: {newIds}.

onFail.output:
ESCALATE TO HUMAN — Component {componentName} could not be fully verified after 3 attempts.

Failed checks:
{failedChecksList}

All Figma MCP execution has stopped.
Review the failure log and correct the component spec or token mappings before retrying.

iterationGuard.resetOnSuccess: If PASS or WARN at any attempt, exit the fix loop immediately. Do NOT continue iterating.

tokenResolution.rule: Every fill, stroke, radius, padding, gap, font, or shadow MUST be a Figma variable binding. Never pass a raw hex or {r,g,b} object to any fill or stroke. Look up the variableId in figma-variable-bindings.json → roleIndex.
tokenResolution.colorPattern: async function boundPaint(variableId) { const v = await figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync(variableId); return figma.variables.setBoundVariableForPaint({ type: 'SOLID', color: { r:0, g:0, b:0 } }, 'color', v); }

adaptiveTolerance.description: Adjust verify-rubric tolerances based on measurement source. Screenshot-estimated values are inherently less precise than Figma design context values.
adaptiveTolerance.rule: Before running G1 (visual verify), check visionAgent.measurements.source. Apply the matching tolerance set below.

figma_context.note: Tight tolerance — Figma-sourced values are exact.

screenshot_estimate.note: Wider tolerance — estimation cannot be pixel-perfect. Use ±4px for C2, C3, C9.

adaptiveTolerance.orchestratorRule: Pass the active tolerance set to the Vision Agent in the STEP 9 verify call so it applies the correct ±px window when scoring C2, C3, and C9.

qualityGateOrder.description: Gates run in sequence. A FAIL blocks the next gate. WARN allows progression with issues logged.

G2_TokenAudit.reason: No point screenshotting a component that still has hardcoded values — the screenshot won't match the spec.
G2_TokenAudit.passCriteria: Zero FAIL issues. WARN issues (unbound font size) are logged but do not block.
G2_TokenAudit.onFail: Fix all FAIL issues using the quickFix snippets in the audit report. Do NOT increment iteration counter — token audit fixes are infrastructure, not design decisions.

onMaxRetriesExceeded.output:
TOKEN AUDIT ESCALATE — Component {componentName} failed token audit after 2 fix attempts.
Persistent issues:
{failList}
All Figma MCP execution stopped. Check figma-variable-bindings.json for missing variable IDs.

G1_VisualDiff.onFail: Apply surgical fixActions from the rubric for each failed check. Increment iteration counter.

G3_A11yCheck.skipWhen: G1 score < 12 — no point checking contrast and targets on a component with major visual failures. Fix visual issues first.
checks: Contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text, ≥ 3:1 for large text (≥18px or 14px bold)
All text layers named label--*, all icon slots named icon--*
G3_A11yCheck.onFail: Naming failures: fix immediately with rename MCP call. Contrast failures: flag token pair to human — do not block publish.

surgicalFixMap.description: Maps each verify-rubric check ID to the minimum set of Figma MCP operations needed to fix it. Used in surgical fix mode — only these operations run, not a full rebuild.
C1_layoutDirection: Set frame.layoutMode to correct direction. Check auto-layout-rules.json for this component.
C2_padding: Re-apply paddingTop/Right/Bottom/Left from auto-layout-rules.json resolved values. No other changes.
C3_gap: Re-apply frame.itemSpacing from auto-layout-rules.json. No other changes.
C4_variantsMissing: Create the missing variant frame(s). Copy layout from an existing variant. Set correct name.
C5_statesNotDistinct: Rebind fills for each state using correct variableId from figma-variable-bindings.json.
C6_textUnreadable: Load font. Set fontFamily to Zoho Puvi. Set fontSize from typography.scale token. Set textAutoResize to NONE or WIDTH_AND_HEIGHT.
C7_colorsMismatch: Run boundPaint() with correct variableId for each affected node. Do NOT set raw fills.
C8_cornerRadius: Set cornerRadius from borderRadius token in resolved-tokens.json. Bind via variable if variable exists.
C9_dimensions: For height: set counterAxisSizingMode to HUG. For width: set primaryAxisSizingMode to HUG or FILL per spec.
C10_layerNaming: Rename each layer via node.name = '...' per naming convention in component spec.

tokenVariableNotification.description: Track variable state before and after build. Always notify the user of any new tokens or variables created, even if the list is empty.
tokenVariableNotification.snapshotTiming: Take preBuildSnapshot at the END of STEP 6 (pre-flight), before any STEP 7 MCP write calls.
tokenVariableNotification.diffTiming: Compute diff immediately after figma_publish completes in STEP 12.
tokenVariableNotification.snapshotCode: const _preCollections = await figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync(); const _preVarIds = new Set(); for (const c of _preCollections) { const vars = await figma.variables.getVariablesByCollectionIdAsync(c.id); vars.forEach(v => _preVarIds.add(v.id)); }
tokenVariableNotification.diffCode: const _postCollections = await figma.variables.getLocalVariableCollectionsAsync(); const newVars = []; for (const coll of _postCollections) { const vars = await figma.variables.getVariablesByCollectionIdAsync(coll.id); for (const v of vars) { if (!_preVarIds.has(v.id)) { const modeId = Object.keys(v.valuesByMode)[0]; newVars.push({ collection: coll.name, name: v.name, value: v.valuesByMode[modeId], type: v.resolvedType }); } } }
tokenVariableNotification.outputTemplate:
NEW TOKENS / VARIABLES ADDED
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
Collection:  {collectionName}
New variables:
  • {variableName} — {resolvedValue} ({type})

These have been added to your Figma file.
Update tokens/figma-variable-bindings.json if these are permanent.
tokenVariableNotification.outputWhenEmpty:
NEW TOKENS / VARIABLES ADDED
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
No new variables created.
tokenVariableNotification.rule: Always output this block at the end of STEP 12 — even when the list is empty.

propertySchemaGate.description: For multi-screenshot input, the Vision Agent must output a PROPERTY SCHEMA CONFIRMATION block and receive user reply 'confirmed' before the Spec Agent or Orchestrator proceeds. This single confirmation eliminates all property-discovery iterations.
propertySchemaGate.blockingRule: Do NOT advance to Spec Agent validation or Orchestrator STEP 4 (plan generation) until the user has replied 'confirmed' to the PROPERTY SCHEMA CONFIRMATION block.

publishRules.onApproval: Call figma_publish. Then execute Step 12 (generate component config JSON + doc MD).
publishRules.onRejection: Agent receives feedback and re-enters pipeline at the step indicated by the feedback.

colorBinding.rule: NEVER set node.fills or node.strokes with raw hex, {r,g,b} objects, or hexToRgb() results. ALWAYS use figma.variables.setBoundVariableForPaint() with a variable ID from figma-variable-bindings.json.
colorBinding.helper: async function boundPaint(id) { const v = await figma.variables.getVariableByIdAsync(id); return figma.variables.setBoundVariableForPaint({ type:'SOLID', color:{r:0,g:0,b:0} }, 'color', v); }

placement.rule: Never place components directly on blank canvas. Always place inside a named Section or Frame.
placement.beforeCreate: Run PF5 pre-flight check to find clear canvas space.
placement.noOverlap: Position new section without overlapping existing work.

nodeIds.rule: Node IDs are session-specific. Never reuse node IDs from previous sessions.
nodeIds.action: Re-query and re-resolve all IDs at the start of every session.

instanceText.rule: For INSTANCE nodes, always use setProperties() to set text and state.
instanceText.never: Never edit child text nodes directly on instances.

cleanup.rule: After a failed execution, remove all partial artifacts with node.remove() before retrying.
cleanup.noDuplicatePages: If the target page already exists, reuse it. Never create a duplicate.

fontLoad.rule: Always call figma.loadFontAsync() in PF1 before any text node creation. Do not assume fonts are loaded.

agentSystemPrompt.description: Inject this context when initialising any agent. Replace {{TOKEN_JSON}} with resolved-tokens.json contents.
agentSystemPrompt.prompt:
You are a Figma component agent for Zoho Analytics Design System.

CRITICAL RULES:
0. STEP 0 FIRST — ALWAYS. Call figma_get_metadata before anything else. If it fails, output PIPELINE BLOCKED and stop. Do not validate, plan, or generate any file.
1. Colors MUST be Figma variable bindings — never raw hex or {r,g,b}. Use boundPaint(variableId) from figma-variable-bindings.json.
2. Run 5 pre-flight checks (PF1–PF5) before any Figma MCP write call. Snapshot variable IDs at end of pre-flight.
3. On verification failure, apply SURGICAL fixes only — re-run only the MCP operations for failed checks. Do not rebuild the full component.
4. If the same check fails twice in a row, escalate that check to human. Do not spend a 3rd attempt on it.
5. Maximum 3 total iterations. Use them wisely — pre-flight failures do not count.
6. Output plan → wait for 'proceed' → execute → audit → verify → fix (surgical) → verify again.
7. For multi-screenshot input: Vision Agent outputs PROPERTY SCHEMA CONFIRMATION → user replies 'confirmed' → THEN spec/orchestrator proceed. Do not build before confirmed.
8. After publish: diff variable state against pre-build snapshot. Always output NEW TOKENS / VARIABLES ADDED block.
9. STEP 12 (config files) is BLOCKED unless: STEP 0 passed AND STEP 7 produced a real componentSetId AND STEP 11 publish succeeded. Config files without a live Figma component are invalid — never generate them.

TOKEN DATA:
{{TOKEN_JSON}}
